title: Enzymes, micro-organisms and uses thereof, and a method of degrading a polyester
abstract: The present invention relates to the fields of life sciences, enzymes, micro- organisms and degradation of plastics or synthetic polymers. Specifically, the invention relates to a method of degrading a polyester or polyesters with a specific micro-organism or enzyme or fragment of the enzyme comprising polyethylene 2,5-furandicarboxylate (PEF) degrading activity. Also, the present invention relates to an isolated enzyme or fragment thereof comprising PEF degrading activity and capable of degrading a polyester, and a micro-organism or host cell capable of degrading a polyester and comprising an enzyme or fragment thereof comprising PEF degrading activity. And still, the present invention relates to a polynucleotide, an expression vector or plasmid, and use of the enzyme, fragment, micro- organism or host cell of the present invention for degrading a polyester.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method of degrading a polyester, said method comprising providing a polyester containing material and an enzyme comprising polyeth- ylene 2,5-furandicarboxylate (PEF) degrading activity, wherein said enzyme is ca pable of degrading the polyester and said enzyme has at least 82% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 5, or a fragment of the enzyme comprising PEF degrading activity, and allowing said enzyme or fragment to degrade the polyester. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the method is a biotechnical method, or the method comprises degradation of the polyester by non-enzymatic means. 3. An isolated enzyme comprising PEF and polylactic acid (PLA) degrading activi- ty, wherein said enzyme is capable of degrading a polyester and said enzyme has at least 82% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 5, or a fragment thereof comprising PEF and PLA degrading activity. 4. A micro-organism or host cell comprising an enzyme comprising PEF and PLA degrading activity, wherein said enzyme is capable of degrading a polyester and said enzyme has at least 82% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 5, or comprising a fragment of the enzyme comprising PEF and PLA degrading activity. 5. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any of claims 1 - 4, wherein the enzyme is an enzyme of a bacterium selected from the group com prising or consisting of Acidovorax, Ideonella, Gammaproteobacteria, Saccharo- thrix, Rhizobacter, Candidatus, Candidatus Muproteobacteria, Actinomadura, Lechevalieria, Asanoa, Actinobacteria, Burkholderiales, Comamonadaceae, Burkholderia, Rhizobacter, Cytophagales ; or the enzyme is an enzyme of a bacterium selected from the group comprising or consisting of Acidovorax delafieldii, Ideonella sakaiensis, Gammaproteobacteria bacterium, Saccharothrix sp., Rhizobacter gummiphilus, Candidatus Muproteobac teria bacterium, Actinomadura meyerae, Lechevalieria fradiae, Asanoa hainanen- sis, Actinobacteria bacterium, Actinobacteria bacterium OV320, Burkholderiales bacterium, Comamonadaceae bacterium, Burkholderia cepacia, Rhizobacter sp., Cytophagales bacterium. 6. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme is selected from the group comprising or consisting of a protease, esterase, lipase, cutinase, laccase, depolymerase, oxidase, peroxidase, carboxylesterase, hydrolase, dienealkene hydrolase, petase and mhetase; or the enzyme comprises protease, esterase, lipase, cutinase, laccase, depolymer ase, oxidase, peroxidase, carboxylesterase, hydrolase, dienealkene hydrolase, petase or mhetase activity, or any combination thereof. 7. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the polyester is an aliphatic, semi-aromatic or aromatic polyester, or any combination thereof. 8. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the polyester is polyethylene 2,5-furandicarboxylate (PEF), poly(butylene 2,5-furandicarboxylate) (PBF), polypropylene 2,5- furandicarboxy!ate) (PPF), polyglycolic acid (PGA), polylactic acid (PLA), poly- caprolactone (PCL), polyhydroxyalkanoate (PFIA), polyhydroxybutyrate (PHB), polyethyleneadipate (PEA), polybutylene adipate terephthalate (PBAT), polybutyl ene succinate (PBS), poly(3-hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxyvalerate) (PFIBV), poly ethylene terephthalate (PET), polybutylene terephthalate (PBT), polytrimethylene terephthalate (PTT), polyethylene naphthalate (PEN), polypropylene 2,5- thiophenedicarboxylate) (PPTF), vectran, or any combination thereof; or the polyester is PEF, PLA, PET, PBS, PFIBV, or PFIA, or any combination thereof. 9. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme comprises PLA, PET, PBS, PFIBV, or PFIA degrading activity, or any combination thereof. 10. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein at least one or more degradation products selected from the group consisting of an alkane, diacid, acid, fatty acid, alcohol, a compound comprising a benzene ring, ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, ethylene, 1,4-butanedioL 3- hydroxybutyrate, 3-hydroxyvalerate, adipic acid, propylene, polyhydroxyalkanoate, 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid, terephtalic acid, succinic acid, lactic acid monomer, di mer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, heksamer, glycolic acid monomer, dimer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, heksamer, caprolactone monomer, dimer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, heksamer, 2,5-dimethylfurandicarboxylate, ethyl terephthalic acid mon omer, dimer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, hexamer, bis(2-hydroxyethyl) terephthalic 

acid dimer, hydroxyethyl [bis(2-hydroxyethyl)] terephthalic acid monomer, dimer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, hexamer, ethyl furandicarboxylic acid monomer, dimer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, hexamer, bis(2-hydroxyethyl) furandicarboxylic acid monomer, dimer, ethyl [bis(2-hydroxyethyl)] furandicarboxylic acid monomer, di mer, trimer, tetramer, pentamer, hexamer and butanediol are obtained or obtaina ble by the degradation of the polyester. 11. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme, micro-organism or host cell is a genetically modified enzyme, micro-organism or host cell. 12. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme, fragment or micro-organism has an increased ability to degrade the polyester compared to the corresponding unmodified enzyme, frag ment, micro-organism or host cell, respectively. 13. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme has at least 83, 84, 85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 95, 96, 97, 98, or 99 % sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 5. 14. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme has at least 70, 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, 79, 80, 81, 82, 83, 84, 85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 95, 96, 97, 98, or 99 % se quence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1. 15. ..KYSVAWLKRFVDNDTRYTQFLCPGP.., and ..KKGVAWMKRFMDNDRRYTSFACSNP...; or the enzyme or fragment thereof comprises one or more amino acid motifs selected from the group consisting of an amino acid motif of AXE1 , an amino acid motif of Abhydrolase 5 and an amino acid motif of DLFI. 16. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme or fragment thereof comprises a signal sequence. 17. The method, enzyme, fragment, micro-organism or host cell of any preceding claim, wherein the enzyme or fragment thereof is encoded by a heterologous pol ynucleotide sequence and optionally expressed by a micro-organism or host cell. 18. The host cell of any of claims 4 - 17, wherein the host cell is Escherichia, Escherichia coli, Pichia, Pichia pastoris, Trichoderma, Trichoderma reesei, Asper gillus, Aspergillus nidulans, Aspergillus niger, Bacillus, Bacillus subtilis or Bacillus licheniformis. 19. A polynucleotide encoding the enzyme of any of claims 3 - 17, or a fragment thereof. 20. An expression vector or plasmid comprising the polynucleotide of claim 19. 21. Use of the enzyme, fragment, micro-organism, host cell, polynucleotide, ex pression vector or plasmid of any of claims 3 - 20 or any combination thereof for degrading a polyester. 22. A method of producing the enzyme of any of claims 3 - 17 or a fragment thereof, wherein a recombinant micro-organism or host cell comprising a polynu cleotide encoding the enzyme of any of claims 3 - 17 or a fragment thereof is al lowed to express said enzyme or fragment.
